segments.text: In the tenth year, in the tenth month on the twelfth day, the word of the Lord came to me:
Son of man, set your face against Pharaoh king of Egypt and prophesy against him and against all Egypt.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: Speak to him and say:
This is what the Sovereign Lord says: I am against you, Pharaoh king of Egypt, you great monster lying among your streams. You say, 'The Nile belongs to me; I made it for myself.'
segments.speaker: The Sovereign Lord

segments.text: But I will put hooks in your jaws and make the fish of your streams stick to your scales. I will pull you out from among your streams, with all the fish sticking to your scales.
segments.speaker: The Sovereign Lord

segments.text: I will leave you in the desert, you and all the fish of your streams. You will fall on the open field and not be gathered or picked up. I will give you as food to the beasts of the earth and the birds of the sky.
segments.speaker: The Sovereign Lord

segments.text: Then all who live in Egypt will know that I am the Lord. You have been a staff of reed for the people of Israel.
segments.speaker: The Sovereign Lord

segments.text: When they grasped you with their hands, you splintered and you tore open their shoulders; when they leaned on you, you broke and their backs were wrenched.
Therefore this is what the Sovereign Lord says: I will bring a sword against you and kill both man and beast.
segments.speaker: The Sovereign Lord

segments.text: Egypt will become a desolate wasteland. Then they will know that I am the Lord. Because you said, 'The Nile is mine; I made it,'
segments.speaker: The Sovereign Lord

segments.text: Therefore I am against you and against your streams, and I will make the land of Egypt a ruin and a desolate waste from Migdol to Aswan, as far as the border of Cush.
segments.speaker: The Sovereign Lord

segments.text: The foot of neither man nor beast will pass through it; no one will live there for forty years.

I will make the land of Egypt desolate among devastated lands, and her cities will lie desolate forty years among ruined cities. And I will disperse the Egyptians among the nations and scatter them through the countries.
segments.speaker: The Sovereign Lord

segments.text: Yet this is what the Sovereign Lord says: At the end of forty years I will gather the Egyptians from the nations where they were scattered.
segments.speaker: The Sovereign Lord

segments.text: I will bring them back from captivity and return them to Upper Egypt, the land of their ancestry. There they will be a lowly kingdom.
segments.speaker: The Sovereign Lord

segments.text: It will be the lowliest of kingdoms and will never again exalt itself above the other nations. I will make it so weak that it will never again rule over the nations.
segments.speaker: The Sovereign Lord

segments.text: Egypt will no longer be a source of confidence for the people of Israel but will be a reminder of their sin in turning to her for help. Then they will know that I am the Sovereign Lord.
segments.speaker: The Sovereign Lord

segments.text: In the twenty-seventh year, in the first month on the first day, the word of the Lord came to me:

Son of man, Nebuchadnezzar king of Babylon drove his army in a hard campaign against Tyre; every head was rubbed bare and every shoulder made raw. Yet he and his army got no reward from the campaign he led against Tyre.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: Therefore this is what the Sovereign Lord says: I am going to give Egypt to Nebuchadnezzar king of Babylon, and he will carry off its wealth. He will loot and plunder the land as pay for his army.
segments.speaker: The Sovereign Lord

segments.text: I have given him Egypt as a reward for his efforts because he and his army did it for me, declares the Sovereign Lord.
segments.speaker: The Sovereign Lord

segments.text: On that day I will make a horn grow for the Israelites, and I will open your mouth among them. Then they will know that I am the Lord.
segments.speaker: The Lord
